rulings.Case Title: JESSE TORRES VS. NEUTRON HOLDINGS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 11 PLAINTIFF JESSE TORRES' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MD TOX LABORATORY, INC. VS. SENNECA DIAGNOSTICS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Yonato M. Rosenzweig
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF MD TOX LABORATORY, INC. DBA INNOVATIVE HEALTH DIAGNOSTICS' Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600; Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to requests for production of documents and special interrogatories is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant must serve responses without objections and produce all responsive documents no later than February 5, 2025. Defendant must pay monetary sanctions of $2,600 no later than February 5, 2025 to plaintiff. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Plaintiff's application for right to attach is denied without prejudice to being refiled based on a fuller record. There is insufficient competent evidence to support the application. Attachment 1 to the application for right to attach filed on December 17, 2024 is a conclusory incomplete/unsigned declaration that does not support the requested relief. While unclear, it appears that counsel Aalaei executed the declaration, but he lacks personal knowledge and thus plaintiff does not satisfy the supporting affidavit requirement of CCP 484.030. In addition, the application lacks a memorandum points and authorities, which would be helpful to establish the required conditions of CCP 483.010(a) and 484.020. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYDIA SEEBACH WENTZ ET AL VS. SEVEN SEAS MUSIC, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFFS' Renewed Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses And Production Of Documents; Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses is granted. Defendants must serve verified further responses without objections to all the document requests and produce all responsive documents no later February 3, 2025. Defendantsand their counsel must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $7,700 to plaintiffs no later than February 3, 2025. Defendants are misusing the discovery process by engaging in a pattern to force Plaintiffs to file motions to compel and then producing documents shortly before the hearing. Plaintiffs' document requests seek discovery relevant, nonprivileged information. Defendants have not argued otherwise. Instead, they claim they have produced everything they can. However, Defendants have not served verified responses to every document request claiming that they have produced all responsive documents in their possession, custody and control. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 8, 1 - DEFENDANT TRAMAINE JOHNSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to all causes of action is sustained with leave to amend as to all causes of action. All causes of action are uncertain. If the plaintiff chooses to file an amended complaint she must do so by February 10, 2025. If she files an amended complaint, plaintiff must provide a short summary of the pertinent facts for each cause of action, and allege, if she can do so in good faith, that she complied with the claims filing requirements of the Government Code or is excused from doing so. rulings.Case Title: YASMIN BAHL VS. DOUGLAS M MOX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 10, 1 - DEFENDANT 1 OF 1 MEDIA, LLC MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Defendant 1 of 1 Media, LLC's motion to quash is continued to May 22, 2025 to allow for jurisdictional discovery. Plaintiff has leave to propound no more than 20 special interrogatories, 20 requests for admission, and five requests for production of documents, and to take no more than 2 depositions of no more than 3 hours each. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed and served no later than April 30, 2025. Supplemental reply papers must be filed and served no later than May 13, 2025. Although plaintiff has not shown sufficient minimum contacts, she should be permitted the opportunity to attempt to do so based on limited jurisdictional discovery. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: YASMIN BAHL VS. DOUGLAS M MOX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 10, 2 - DEFENDANT DOUGLAS MOX, 1 OF 1 MEDIA, LLC, CHIPS BRAND, LLC MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YASMIN BAHL VS. DOUGLAS M MOX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT DOUGLAS MOX, CHIPS BRAND, LLC'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, line 10, 4 - DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS MOX, CHIPS BRAND, LLC'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Mox and Chips Brand, LLC's demurrer to all six causes of action is overruled as to the first, second, third, fourth and fifth causes of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff may file an amended complaint no later than February 13, 2025. Plaintiff, Yasmin Bahl, is suing Douglas M. Mox for fraud after she invested over a million dollars in his company, and never received the money back or any interest she was promised. Plaintiff is also suing the businesses she invested in. Specifically, Plaintiff alleges causes of action for (1) fraud, (2) negligent misrepresentation, (3) omission of facts, (4) violations of the corporations code sections 25501 and 25503, (5) violation of corporations code section 25110, (6) quantum meruit/unjust enrichment. Plaintiff requests return of the montey that she invested in the company, interest, and attorney's fees and costs. Defendants' argument that the statute of limitations bars plaintiff's claims is not amenable on this pleading motion since the face of the complaint fails to show that any of the claims are necessarily time-barred. Defendants' argument that the complaint fails to join a necessary party lacks merit because the face of the complaint does not establish that a necessary party is missing. The third cause of action for "omission of facts" adequately alleges a cause of action for fraud by failure to disclose. The sixth cause of action fails to allege the required element that a third party received property that a defendant knew or had reason to know was fraudulently obtained. (CACI 375.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)
